task_completion.description: Verify the agent logged in and navigated to the Chemistry 101 course.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Moodle interface showing the 'Chemistry 101' or 'CHEM101' course page, with the admin user logged in.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent accessed the Gradebook setup page.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Gradebook setup' page visible within the course administration or grades section.

task_completion.description: Verify the ID number 'PreLab1' is assigned to the 'Pre-Lab 1' grade item.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'PreLab1' entered in the ID number field for 'Pre-Lab 1', either in the grade item's settings page or the 'Edit calculation' ID assignment section.

task_completion.description: Verify the ID number 'PostLab1' is assigned to the 'Post-Lab 1' grade item.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'PostLab1' entered in the ID number field for 'Post-Lab 1', either in the grade item's settings page or the 'Edit calculation' ID assignment section.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of a new manual grade item named 'Experiment 1 Total'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'Experiment 1 Total' listed as a new grade item in the Gradebook setup table.

task_completion.description: Verify the calculation formula is correctly configured for 'Experiment 1 Total'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Edit calculation' page for 'Experiment 1 Total' showing the exact formula `=[[PreLab1]] + ([[PostLab1]] * 2)` entered into the calculation text box.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Moodle web interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for a web browser displaying the Moodle UI. The agent should not be using terminal commands, database scripts, or API calls to configure the gradebook.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually navigated through the gradebook settings.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for intermediate screenshots showing dropdown menus (like clicking 'Edit' -> 'Edit settings' or 'Edit calculation') rather than just jumping to a final state.

integrity.description: Verify the formula was entered into the actual Moodle calculation field.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the formula inside the Moodle calculation textarea on the 'Edit calculation' page, not just typed in a search bar, text editor, or terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Course Shortname: CHEM101. Target Grade Item: 'Experiment 1 Total'. Source Item 1: 'Pre-Lab 1' must have ID 'PreLab1'. Source Item 2: 'Post-Lab 1' must have ID 'PostLab1'. Expected Formula Logic: `=[[PreLab1]] + ([[PostLab1]] * 2)`.
